summary.decision_made: Locked Phase 1 gameplay loop for the pure-chance drinking-game race. Single round = Lobby → Bidding → Setup → Race → Settlement → Distribution → Ready. 9 phases total. Bidding capped 1–5, all bids public, 30s distribution timer with uniform-random fallback, 4 horses, track 6–20.
options_considered: Pure-chance (chosen) vs. light influence lever for V1
Full hidden bids vs. public bids (chose public for social drama)
Bid cap 1–5 (chosen) vs. free-amount vs. multi-horse split
Random fallback policy: uniform random (chosen) vs. equal split vs. lowest-consumption target
summary.difficulties_encountered: Track-card flip rule (last-placed horse triggers) creates an asymmetric early/late-race dynamic; explicitly documented in Core Loop Step 5 and Glossary 'Step (last-placed horse triggers flip)' to avoid future confusion.
summary.key_takeaway: V1 is a pure-luck betting game where the only meaningful choice is bid-amount + post-race drinks distribution; deck-builder effect cards are explicitly deferred to V2.

summary.decision_made: Created a dedicated Future Concept Vault at .context/race_modifiers.md to capture expansion ideas (Energy Channeling, Track modifiers, Suit Synchronization) without scope-creeping the V1 prototype. AGENTS.md updated with a structured interview protocol for capturing and refining future modifier pitches.
options_considered: Dedicated vault file with structured schema (field descriptions, beneficiary, upgrades) — chosen
Keep ideas flattened in todo_random.md — rejected (too unstructured, no upgrade paths)
No formal capture, rely on memory — rejected (ideas get lost)
summary.difficulties_encountered: Balancing a schema structured enough for future design work but lightweight enough not to create friction during brainstorming. The current schema uses a 'fast-track' bypass clause for half-baked ideas.
summary.reversals_and_refactors: N/A — first implementation of this process.
summary.key_takeaway: Formalizing an ideation vault early prevents promising expansion concepts from either being forgotten or prematurely derailing the core prototype.
